issues.title: Function to load model on Spring Ollama
issues.body:
I would like to request a function to load and check if a model is loaded. Write now, when I boot the project and request anything I just get an error:
[404] Not Found - {"error":"model 'mistral' not found, try pulling it first"}
It only starts working after I run the below:
```
curl http://localhost:11434/api/pull -d '{    
"name": "mistral"  
}'
```

**Expected Behavior**

Just a function to load models and check if they were loaded or exist.

**Current Behavior**

Doesn't exist a way to check inside the spring project if the model were loaded or not, or an option to load it.

**Context**

Now I still need to go to the terminal to load the model outside the project. 
Here is the repository I'm playing with: https://github.com/Adrianogba/Spring-Ollama

I have the same problem, I need to download this model before I can use it
```
org.springframework.ai.ollama.api.OllamaApi$OllamaResponseErrorHandler.handleError(OllamaApi.java:78)
Not Found - {"error":"model 'mistral' not found, try pulling it first"}
```

@Adrianogba , @mingMens as [Ollama documentation explains](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chat/ollama-chat.html#_prerequisites) you need to `pull` and `run` the models (you can run more than one) before using it.

```
ollama pull mistral
ollama run mistral
```

No the model is served and ready to use by your application.

Having said this, I can see that allowing to check if the model is pulled. But the Ollama API doesn't allow running models so you still have to do it from the command line?

There is no need to "run" a model in Ollama, a simple "pull" is sufficient. Whenever Ollama receives a request for a certain model, it will automatically run that model if not already running (as long as it was pulled already).

Ollama provides APIs to manage models, including listing, copying, deleting, and pulling models. In this PR, I have extended the Spring AI Ollama module to offer access to those APIs.

https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/pull/1532

Based on that change, it will be possible to introduce an option in Spring AI to have the application pull the necessary Ollama models at startup time, if they have not been pulled already. That's what I'm investigating right now. I'll share some results soon with possible solutions.

It would be a great developer experience improvement, making the application self-contained both when using native Ollama and Ollama Testcontainers. It would also enable interesting scenarios to design applications where the end-user could switch between the different available models in Ollama from the application UI.
